cards.grammarNote: zéro is invariable — it never changes form.
un (masculine) / une (feminine). In compound numbers above 20, 'et un / et une' is used for x1: vingt et un, vingt et une.
The teens 11–16 are unique words (onze, douze, treize, quatorze, quinze, seize). Teens 17–19 are compounds: dix-sept, dix-huit, dix-neuf.
French has no single word for 70. It is soixante (60) + dix (10). Continue the pattern: 71 = soixante et onze, 72 = soixante-douze … 79 = soixante-dix-neuf.
quatre-vingts = 4 × 20. The final s is dropped in compounds: quatre-vingt-un (81), quatre-vingt-dix (90). No 'et' is ever used in the 80s.
quatre-vingt-dix = 4×20 + 10. Continue: 91 = quatre-vingt-onze, 99 = quatre-vingt-dix-neuf. No 'et' in the 90s either.
cent takes an s when it stands alone as a round number (deux cents = 200) but loses it in compounds: deux cent cinq (205).
Age uses avoir (to have), not être (to be): j'ai … ans = I have … years. Never say *Je suis vingt-cinq ans*.
'Et' is used only for x1 in the tens 21–71: vingt et un, trente et un, quarante et un, cinquante et un, soixante et un, soixante et onze. No other tens use 'et'.
For x2 through x9, use a hyphen directly: vingt-deux, vingt-trois … vingt-neuf. No 'et', no space.
Euro is masculine: un euro, deux euros. The number comes before the currency: quatre-vingt-dix-neuf euros.

cards.back: vingt et un livres / vingt et une tables
cards.grammarNote: The 'un/une' in compound numbers agrees with the noun: vingt et un livres (masc.) vs vingt et une tables (fem.). This applies to all x1 compounds.
